compounddef.basecompoundref: nanoflann::KDTreeBaseClass< KDTreeSingleIndexAdaptor< Distance, DatasetAdaptor, -1, uint32_t >, Distance, DatasetAdaptor, -1, uint32_t >

memberdef.definition: bool nanoflann::KDTreeSingleIndexAdaptor< Distance, DatasetAdaptor, DIM, IndexType >::findNeighbors
memberdef.name: findNeighbors

detaileddescription.para: Find set of nearest neighbors to vec[0:dim-1]. Their indices are stored inside the result object.
Params: result = the result object in which the indices of the nearest-neighbors are stored vec = the vector for which to search the nearest neighbors

simplesect.para: True if the requested neighbors could be found.
If L2 norms are used, all returned distances are actually squared distances.

memberdef.definition: Size nanoflann::KDTreeSingleIndexAdaptor< Distance, DatasetAdaptor, DIM, IndexType >::knnSearch
memberdef.argsstring: (const ElementType *query_point, const Size num_closest, IndexType *out_indices, DistanceType *out_distances) const
memberdef.name: knnSearch

detaileddescription.para: Find the "num_closest" nearest neighbors to the query_point[0:dim-1]. Their indices and distances are stored in the provided pointers to array/vector.

simplesect.para: Number N of valid points in the result set.
If L2 norms are used, all returned distances are actually squared distances.
Only the first N entries in out_indices and out_distances will be valid. Return is less than num_closest only if the number of elements in the tree is less than num_closest.

memberdef.definition: Size nanoflann::KDTreeSingleIndexAdaptor< Distance, DatasetAdaptor, DIM, IndexType >::radiusSearch
memberdef.argsstring: (const ElementType *query_point, const DistanceType &radius, std::vector< ResultItem< IndexType, DistanceType >> &IndicesDists, const SearchParameters &searchParams={}) const
memberdef.name: radiusSearch

detaileddescription.para: Find all the neighbors to query_point[0:dim-1] within a maximum radius. The output is given as a vector of pairs, of which the first element is a point index and the second the corresponding distance. Previous contents of IndicesDists are cleared.
If searchParams.sorted==true, the output list is sorted by ascending distances.
For a better performance, it is advisable to do a .reserve() on the vector if you have any wild guess about the number of expected matches.

simplesect.para: The number of points within the given radius (i.e. indices.size() or dists.size() )
If L2 norms are used, search radius and all returned distances are actually squared distances.

memberdef.definition: Size nanoflann::KDTreeSingleIndexAdaptor< Distance, DatasetAdaptor, DIM, IndexType >::radiusSearchCustomCallback
memberdef.name: radiusSearchCustomCallback

detaileddescription.para:
Just like radiusSearch() but with a custom callback class for each point found in the radius of the query. See the source of RadiusResultSet<> as a start point for your own classes.
radiusSearch

memberdef.definition: Size nanoflann::KDTreeSingleIndexAdaptor< Distance, DatasetAdaptor, DIM, IndexType >::rknnSearch
memberdef.argsstring: (const ElementType *query_point, const Size num_closest, IndexType *out_indices, DistanceType *out_distances, const DistanceType &radius) const
memberdef.name: rknnSearch

detaileddescription.para: Find the first N neighbors to query_point[0:dim-1] within a maximum radius. The output is given as a vector of pairs, of which the first element is a point index and the second the corresponding distance. Previous contents of IndicesDists are cleared.

simplesect.para: Number N of valid points in the result set.
If L2 norms are used, all returned distances are actually squared distances.
Only the first N entries in out_indices and out_distances will be valid. Return is less than num_closest only if the number of elements in the tree is less than num_closest.

detaileddescription.para: The data source used by this index

memberdef.definition: nanoflann::KDTreeSingleIndexAdaptor< Distance, DatasetAdaptor, DIM, IndexType >::KDTreeSingleIndexAdaptor
memberdef.name: KDTreeSingleIndexAdaptor

param.type: const KDTreeSingleIndexAdaptor< Distance, DatasetAdaptor, DIM, IndexType > &

detaileddescription.para: Deleted copy constructor

memberdef.definition: nanoflann::KDTreeSingleIndexAdaptor< Distance, DatasetAdaptor, DIM, IndexType >::KDTreeSingleIndexAdaptor
memberdef.argsstring: (const Dimension dimensionality, const DatasetAdaptor &inputData, const KDTreeSingleIndexAdaptorParams &params, Args &&... args)
memberdef.name: KDTreeSingleIndexAdaptor

detaileddescription.para: KDTree constructor
Refer to docs in README.md or online in https://github.com/jlblancoc/nanoflann

The KD-Tree point dimension (the length of each point in the datase, e.g. 3 for 3D points) is determined by means of:
The DIM template parameter if >0 (highest priority)
Otherwise, the dimensionality parameter of this constructor.

inputData
Dataset with the input features. Its lifetime must be equal or longer than that of the instance of this class.
params
Basically, the maximum leaf node size
Note that there is a variable number of optional additional parameters which will be forwarded to the metric class constructor. Refer to example examples/pointcloud_custom_metric.cpp for a use case.

memberdef.definition: nanoflann::KDTreeSingleIndexAdaptor< Distance, DatasetAdaptor, DIM, IndexType >::KDTreeSingleIndexAdaptor
memberdef.argsstring: (const Dimension dimensionality, const DatasetAdaptor &inputData, const KDTreeSingleIndexAdaptorParams &params={})
memberdef.name: KDTreeSingleIndexAdaptor

memberdef.definition: void nanoflann::KDTreeSingleIndexAdaptor< Distance, DatasetAdaptor, DIM, IndexType >::buildIndex
memberdef.name: buildIndex

detaileddescription.para: Builds the index

memberdef.definition: void nanoflann::KDTreeSingleIndexAdaptor< Distance, DatasetAdaptor, DIM, IndexType >::init_vind
memberdef.name: init_vind

detaileddescription.para: Make sure the auxiliary list vind has the same size than the current dataset, and re-generate if size has changed.

memberdef.definition: void nanoflann::KDTreeSingleIndexAdaptor< Distance, DatasetAdaptor, DIM, IndexType >::computeBoundingBox
memberdef.name: computeBoundingBox

memberdef.definition: bool nanoflann::KDTreeSingleIndexAdaptor< Distance, DatasetAdaptor, DIM, IndexType >::searchLevel
memberdef.argsstring: (RESULTSET &result_set, const ElementType *vec, const NodePtr node, DistanceType mindist, distance_vector_t &dists, const float epsError) const
memberdef.name: searchLevel

detaileddescription.para:
Performs an exact search in the tree starting from a node.
RESULTSET
Should be any ResultSet<DistanceType>
true if the search should be continued, false if the results are sufficient

memberdef.definition: void nanoflann::KDTreeSingleIndexAdaptor< Distance, DatasetAdaptor, DIM, IndexType >::saveIndex
memberdef.name: saveIndex

detaileddescription.para:
Stores the index in a binary file. IMPORTANT NOTE: The set of data points is NOT stored in the file, so when loading the index object it must be constructed associated to the same source of data points used while building it. See the example: examples/saveload_example.cpp
loadIndex

memberdef.definition: void nanoflann::KDTreeSingleIndexAdaptor< Distance, DatasetAdaptor, DIM, IndexType >::loadIndex
memberdef.name: loadIndex

detaileddescription.para:
Loads a previous index from a binary file. IMPORTANT NOTE: The set of data points is NOT stored in the file, so the index object must be constructed associated to the same source of data points used while building the index. See the example: examples/saveload_example.cpp
loadIndex

memberdef.definition: void nanoflann::KDTreeSingleIndexAdaptor< Distance, DatasetAdaptor, DIM, IndexType >::init
memberdef.name: init

detaileddescription.para: kd-tree static index
Contains the k-d trees and other information for indexing a set of points for nearest-neighbor matching.
The class "DatasetAdaptor" must provide the following interface (can be non-virtual, inlined methods):

parameterdescription.para: The user-provided adaptor, which must be ensured to have a lifetime equal or longer than the instance of this class.
The distance metric to use: nanoflann::metric_L1, nanoflann::metric_L2, nanoflann::metric_L2_Simple, etc.
Dimensionality of data points (e.g. 3 for 3D points)
Will be typically size_t or int
